title: Special Relationship

jokes.text: We are running some reverse-'My Fair Lady' shit here. We're showing she's a regular gal.
jokes.setup: High-stakes political image management
jokes.punchline: Describing sophisticated political theater as 'reverse My Fair Lady shit'
jokes.explanation: Smart cultural reference twisted into political spin-speak, economical and character-appropriate

jokes.text: 'Good Mourning, America'... mourning with a 'u.'
jokes.setup: Memorial service coverage needs a catchy headline
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'Good Morning America' but explaining the wordplay kills it
jokes.explanation: Weak pun made worse by over-explanation, typical of hack political operative thinking

jokes.text: that 65-year-old fuckin' intern
jokes.setup: Prince Charles being discussed as important dignitary to meet
jokes.punchline: Dismissing heir to throne as elderly intern
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reduction of royalty to office hierarchy, perfect economy and attitude

jokes.text: He had tickets for a West End musical. Something with a duck or a ghost. I don't remember.
jokes.setup: POTUS couldn't attend important memorial
jokes.punchline: Vague dismissal of his cultural activities shows her self-absorption
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Selina's dismissive ignorance of cultural details

jokes.text: Hey, is FLOTUS having an affair? Not that I would blame her.
jokes.setup: Casual conversation about the First Lady
jokes.punchline: Immediately jumping to affair speculation with fake sympathy
jokes.explanation: Dark political gossip delivered with faux concern, very Selina

jokes.text: Her neck is like stretched cheese. Yeah, it looks bad now. Bad. It's gone bad.
jokes.setup: FLOTUS has lost weight
jokes.punchline: Increasingly harsh physical description escalating to 'gone bad'
jokes.explanation: Escalating cruelty about appearance, building to food spoilage metaphor

jokes.text: Heroin and Chinese food. Noodles and needles.
jokes.setup: Mixing up meeting locations
jokes.punchline: Alliterative pairing of drugs and food
jokes.explanation: Forced wordplay trying too hard to be clever about Hong Kong stereotypes

jokes.text: Same vibe.
jokes.setup: Corrected about meeting being Stockholm solar summit, not Hong Kong
jokes.punchline: Dismisses correction with casual 'same vibe'
jokes.explanation: Perfect politician deflection, economical and in character

jokes.text: I'm nailing pathos to the fricking wall here. Death, glory, folly, tragedy. It's got the four main mood groups.
jokes.setup: Mike describing his WWI memorial speech
jokes.punchline: Reducing complex emotions to 'four main mood groups' like a mood ring
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mike - overwrought self-importance reduced to pseudo-scientific categories

jokes.text: It's like watching a goat trying to use an ATM.
jokes.setup: Ray talking to Bank of England official
jokes.punchline: Vivid animal metaphor for intellectual mismatch
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual metaphor, perfect economy, highly quotable and specific

jokes.text: Because money is just a concept. We believe in it because we're too scared not to. There's no intrinsic value to it. It's not like muscles in your arms.
jokes.setup: Ray trying to sound intellectual at Bank of England
jokes.punchline: Pseudo-profound nonsense ending with bizarre muscle comparison
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ray character moment - trying too hard to sound deep, ending absurdly

jokes.text: I try to learn a word a day. I love words very ardently.
jokes.setup: Ray claiming intellectual sophistication
jokes.punchline: Awkward use of 'ardently' while claiming to love words
jokes.explanation: Ray's pseudo-intellectualism shown through clunky word choice

jokes.text: And I hope that 'honestly' isn't one of those words that's lost its meaning whilst traveling the Atlantic.
jokes.setup: American says 'honestly' about security decision
jokes.punchline: Elegant British skepticism about American honesty
jokes.explanation: Sophisticated diplomatic burn with perfect British understatement

jokes.text: Because the U.S. doesn't spy on its allies. Now I'm relieved. We collect data. Same thing. Oh, no.
jokes.setup: Diplomatic dance about spying allegations
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction of semantic distinction
jokes.explanation: Good diplomatic doublespeak exposed immediately

jokes.text: Gary likes to keep his cards close to his chest.
jokes.setup: Gary asked for opinion in diplomatic situation
jokes.punchline: Selina covering for Gary's obvious lack of anything to say
jokes.explanation: Standard deflection for Gary's incompetence, in character but predictable

jokes.text: I'm just 'collecting data.'
jokes.setup: Previous spying vs data collection distinction
jokes.punchline: Using their own euphemism against them
jokes.explanation: Good callback turning their euphemism into a weapon

jokes.text: Um... I think it's very nuanced. And I think there's a lot of different sides to a lot of different topics that are out there that I think we could spend some time...
jokes.setup: Gary finally asked to give opinion on complex diplomatic issue
jokes.punchline: Rambling non-answer that says absolutely nothing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary moment - trying to sound thoughtful while saying nothing

jokes.text: Gary, I need that lipstick.
jokes.setup: Gary's endless rambling diplomatic non-answer
jokes.punchline: Selina cutting him off with mundane assistant task
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing - diplomatic crisis interrupted by lipstick needs

jokes.text: Madam out there hobnobbing, us here in the servants' quarters. It's like 'Downton Abbey.'
jokes.setup: Staff separated from Selina at fancy event
jokes.punchline: Comparing their situation to upstairs/downstairs TV drama
jokes.explanation: Decent cultural reference but fairly obvious comparison

jokes.text: McClintock, Lady Selina wishes to eat a Cornish pasty in the paddock this evening. Post-haste, you bugger!
jokes.setup: Downton Abbey comparison established
jokes.punchline: Mike doing exaggerated British servant voice
jokes.explanation: Mike trying physical comedy, moderately amusing but not exceptional

jokes.text: I love that voice. It makes me feel inadequate and horny. The dream.
jokes.setup: Mike's British accent performance
jokes.punchline: Gary's inappropriate sexual response to authority
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Gary - inappropriate overshare revealing psychological quirks

jokes.text: '60-Day Shred.' 'Get A Bod Like God,' 'My Name Is Ray, You're Okay,' 'The Greater Glory In 20 Days'
jokes.setup: Ray asked about his published titles
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous self-help book titles
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of absurd self-help titles, very in character for Ray

jokes.text: Wasn't it Oscar Wilde who said, 'Dan's a fucking terrible campaign manager'?
jokes.setup: Literary reference format
jokes.punchline: Attributing modern insult to classic author
jokes.explanation: Clever format joke mixing high culture with office politics, very quotable

jokes.text: I would like to shoot him, but there are no guns in this country.
jokes.setup: Amy's frustration with Dan
jokes.punchline: Casual murder wish thwarted by gun laws
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about American gun culture, economical and in character

jokes.text: I'm in London Town, and it's going to be a 'right royal cockney barrel of turnips' when I dish the dirt on Selina Meyer.
jokes.setup: Jonah trying to sound like spy reporting in
jokes.punchline: Mangled fake British expressions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah - trying too hard with fake cultural knowledge

jokes.text: I can imagine Harry Potter getting loaded in here.
jokes.setup: Commenting on charming British pub
jokes.punchline: Imagining fictional child character drinking
jokes.explanation: Typical Selina inappropriate cultural reference, moderately funny

jokes.text: Well, he's a small child, so, underage.
jokes.setup: Selina's Harry Potter drinking comment
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact correction about fictional child's legal status
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response, economical and appropriately confused

jokes.text: Although I think the actor is quite a grown man. The actor, yes. But the original, the literary version, which, of course, is the fictional story of a child witch. So... would very much still be considered a minor. Right. Actually, I think it's a wizard.
jokes.setup: Harry Potter age discussion
jokes.punchline: Increasingly awkward over-explanation ending with wizard/witch correction
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment but overly long for the payoff, typical Selina digging deeper

jokes.text: 'Jonah Bond, double-o-fuck off.'
jokes.setup: Jonah in London to spy
jokes.punchline: James Bond parody with profane twist
jokes.explanation: Solid spy parody with good economy and quotability factor

jokes.text: What kind of ham did you say?
jokes.setup: Pub owner mentions being West Ham fan
jokes.punchline: Selina thinks he's talking about literal ham
jokes.explanation: Classic American ignorance of British culture, very Selina but predictable

jokes.text: We call that soccer, actually. No, here, football. Right. We have football there, and I'm wondering what do you call what we call soccer? That's one thing I don't... Yeah. Football.
jokes.setup: Football vs soccer confusion
jokes.punchline: Selina getting increasingly confused by simple concept
jokes.explanation: Predictable cultural confusion joke that goes on too long, but in character

jokes.text: I'm joking. He won't. I think you look like a bitter person, maybe.
jokes.setup: Offering pinot noir at British pub
jokes.punchline: 'Bitter' as beer type vs personality assessment
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay on beer terminology, mild but functional

jokes.text: The vice president is now going to 'pull a pint.' Come on down. I just said that.
jokes.setup: Mike announcing photo op
jokes.punchline: Repeating exactly what pub owner just said
jokes.explanation: Typical Mike redundancy, in character but not particularly strong

jokes.text: 'Daniwah!' Okay! 'Daniwah!' Daniwah! Daniwah!
jokes.setup: Crowd chanting 'down in one' for drinking
jokes.punchline: Selina mishearing and repeating nonsense word enthusiastically
jokes.explanation: Great cringe moment that becomes running gag, highly quotable and memorable

jokes.text: They're eating this up. I think we just landed a catchphrase.
jokes.setup: Selina chanting 'Daniwah' with crowd
jokes.punchline: Dan completely misreading the situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dan overconfidence completely missing the point

jokes.text: They're not saying 'Daniwah.' They're saying 'down in one.' Moron.
jokes.setup: Dan's misinterpretation of crowd chant
jokes.punchline: Amy's brutal correction with insult
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man correction with Amy's typical directness

jokes.text: They're laughing at her like a toddler they taught to swear.
jokes.setup: Selina enthusiastically chanting 'Daniwah'
jokes.punchline: Perfect metaphor for condescending amusement
jokes.explanation: Brilliant, specific metaphor that perfectly captures the dynamic, highly quotable

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. Like the Hindenburg.
jokes.setup: Gary asking if she's feeling gassy
jokes.punchline: Comparing her gas to famous disaster
jokes.explanation: Good historical reference for bodily function, economical and memorable

jokes.text: Okay, 'Daniwah' is trending. It's number 2 under Kate Middleton's bony ass.
jokes.setup: Social media reaction to pub visit
jokes.punchline: Casual insult about royal family member's appearance
jokes.explanation: Good callback with social media reality, casual royal insult

jokes.text: You know, I thought it was some sort of a Japanese thing that he was saying. Yeah, like 'konnichiwa.'
jokes.setup: Explaining the 'Daniwah' misunderstanding
jokes.punchline: Thinking British chant was Japanese greeting
jokes.explanation: Reasonable explanation for mishearing, shows cultural confusion

jokes.text: Siri, how many horses died in the First World War?
jokes.setup: Mike working on WWI memorial speech
jokes.punchline: Asking phone for obscure historical statistics about horses
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mike moment - overthinking speech with irrelevant details

jokes.text: Those guys are gonna wish they weren't dead.
jokes.setup: Saying Selina will look stunning at war memorial
jokes.punchline: Suggesting dead soldiers would want to be alive to see her
jokes.explanation: Darkly inappropriate but perfectly Gary - sexualizing solemn memorial

jokes.text: Don't you think the other hat is a little too 'hatty'?
jokes.setup: Choosing between hats for memorial service
jokes.punchline: Inventing nonsense adjective 'hatty' for hat
jokes.explanation: Typical Selina making up words, mildly amusing and quotable

jokes.text: Britain is the kingdom of the hats. I have never heard that phrase. Is it?
jokes.setup: Gary making up British cultural fact
jokes.punchline: Selina immediately questioning obvious fabrication
jokes.explanation: Good Gary moment trying to sound knowledgeable, caught immediately

jokes.text: Or nothing at all. Ooh... what? I don't know what you mean.
jokes.setup: Discussing what else Selina could wear hat with
jokes.punchline: Gary accidentally suggesting nudity, immediate awkward backtrack
jokes.explanation: Classic Gary inappropriate comment with panic response

jokes.text: I am 'Dan With A Plan' Egan, so if I can't work, then you can't work
jokes.setup: Dan trying to rally staff for crisis management
jokes.punchline: Giving himself ridiculous nickname
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dan ego moment, self-aggrandizing nickname is very him

jokes.text: That's... I was doing a little role-playing. Like the Charles Manson defense?
jokes.setup: Dan explaining his dramatic behavior
jokes.punchline: Kent comparing it to infamous murderer's legal strategy
jokes.explanation: Dark Kent reference, perfectly delivered deadpan comparison

jokes.text: Nicely done, 'Obi-Wah Kenobi.'
jokes.setup: Dan in newspaper coverage
jokes.punchline: Star Wars reference mixed with 'Daniwah' callback
jokes.explanation: Clever combination of pop culture and running gag, economical

jokes.text: I can chop this thing in two like a disputed kitten.
jokes.setup: Dan claiming he can handle one bad newspaper
jokes.punchline: Bizarre violent metaphor involving kitten
jokes.explanation: Memorably absurd metaphor, darkly funny and very quotable

jokes.text: As I do everything I write for you.
jokes.setup: Mike claiming he gave speech 100% effort
jokes.punchline: Defensive addition implying he usually doesn't try
jokes.explanation: Mike accidentally revealing his usual lack of effort, self-sabotaging

jokes.text: It's just a little bit too 'effy' for me, do you know?
jokes.setup: Criticizing Mike's poetic WWI speech
jokes.punchline: Reducing literary alliteration to dismissive 'effy'
jokes.explanation: Good Selina dismissal of sophistication with made-up word

jokes.text: I could 'normalfy' this for you.
jokes.setup: Ray offering to help with speech accessibility
jokes.punchline: Making up word 'normalfy' while trying to sound helpful
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ray - trying to sound smart but inventing words

jokes.text: Everything was much easier when it was just floppy discs.
jokes.setup: Confusion about iCloud file access
jokes.punchline: Mike longing for obsolete technology
jokes.explanation: Good Mike tech confusion moment, relatable generational humor

jokes.text: There's a whole lotta guys who never came home. Good guys. Here's to those guys.
jokes.setup: Ray offering alternative to Mike's eloquent WWI speech
jokes.punchline: Reducing complex memorial to caveman-level simplicity
jokes.explanation: Hilariously reductive speech attempt, perfect Ray dumbing-down

jokes.text: Holy fuckin' Christ. What is that, Ray?
jokes.setup: Ray's terrible speech rewrite
jokes.punchline: Mike's horror at his life's work being demolished
jokes.explanation: Good Mike reaction to professional insult, earned outrage

jokes.text: What? Were you there the whole time? Yeah, yeah.
jokes.setup: Selina looking for Gary who responds from right there
jokes.punchline: Gary was invisibly present during entire conversation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary invisibility moment, good character comedy beat

jokes.text: Oh, thank you. Actually, I don't run it anymore. Oh, what a shame. I was going to give you a story about Ray Whelans. You know, I do still run it.
jokes.setup: Reporter praising Jonah's website
jokes.punchline: Jonah immediately contradicting himself when story is offered
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah flip-flopping for personal gain, very characteristic

jokes.text: I feel like Shakespeare when that monkey tried to make fun of him. What? I think that might have been a cartoon.
jokes.setup: Mike comparing himself to Shakespeare
jokes.punchline: Amy pointing out he's thinking of fictional scenario
jokes.explanation: Good Mike intellectual pretension deflated by Amy's reality check

jokes.text: I'll hold off that long streak of deputy piss while she's in there.
jokes.setup: Ben managing Selina's meeting with German chancellor
jokes.punchline: Reducing Deputy PM to 'streak of deputy piss'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Ben insult, creative and economical put-down

jokes.text: Let's remember the guys who never came home. Here's to the good guys. Boy, they were good guys, weren't they? They were.
jokes.setup: Formal memorial service with solemn speech
jokes.punchline: Selina using Ray's terrible casual version
jokes.explanation: Great callback showing Selina's poor judgment in solemn moment

jokes.text: I'm not waiting on a guy to have sex with. I am waiting on a guy to talk... he's a reporter.
jokes.setup: Construction worker asking if Jonah lost his car
jokes.punchline: Jonah's panicked over-explanation about not waiting for sex
jokes.explanation: Typical Jonah overcompensation creating more awkwardness

jokes.text: I don't know, I just thought 'Watergate, Deep Throat.'
jokes.setup: Reporter asking why meet in parking garage
jokes.punchline: Jonah's naive reference to famous journalism meeting
jokes.explanation: Good Jonah trying to be dramatic journalist, predictable but in character

jokes.text: Eight pounds for two coffees? Thank you, America.
jokes.setup: Paying for expensive London coffee
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic thanks to America for... expensive British prices?
jokes.explanation: Weak price complaint joke that doesn't quite land logically

jokes.text: No, I've got a super hot British source with a super sexy voice. She sounds like Mary Poppins on all fours.
jokes.setup: Reporter uninterested in fitness trainer story
jokes.punchline: Jonah sexualizing children's character in disturbing way
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable Jonah sexuality, characteristic but cringe-inducing

jokes.text: You do realize Mary Poppins would be over 100 by now? Yeah, and I'd love to sweep her chimney, eh?
jokes.setup: Logical point about fictional character's age
jokes.punchline: Jonah doubling down with crude sexual metaphor
jokes.explanation: Jonah pushing inappropriate joke further, cheap sexual humor

jokes.text: So you Googled it. I didn't just Google it. I found this thing called the 'Internet Archive'... So you Googled it.
jokes.setup: Jonah claiming sophisticated research methods
jokes.punchline: Reporter reducing everything to simple Googling
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Jonah's self-importance, effective repetition

jokes.text: Obesity is a punishment for sins committed in a previous life. Fat people have to learn self-discipline until they are rid of the demon that lives within them.
jokes.setup: Jonah revealing Ray's controversial blog post
jokes.punchline: Absurdly offensive pseudo-spiritual diet advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ray pseudo-spirituality taken to offensive extreme

jokes.text: Right. I saw the German chancellor just now heading toward the Lady Chapel. Yeah. He was going in there to pray privately.
jokes.setup: Asked about Selina's whereabouts with obvious lie
jokes.punchline: Continuing transparent lie about chancellor's activities
jokes.explanation: Diplomatic lying sequence, functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: Yes... ja, as they say in Germany.
jokes.setup: Deputy PM asking if Ben speaks German
jokes.punchline: Ben's obvious attempt to sound knowledgeable with basic word
jokes.explanation: Good Ben attempting sophistication with minimal knowledge

jokes.text: Ich muss wieder zur schule gehen. Absolutely.
jokes.setup: Deputy PM testing Ben's German with complex sentence
jokes.punchline: Ben agreeing without understanding
jokes.explanation: Good trap for Ben's fake language skills, well executed

jokes.text: I said, 'I should go back to school.' Yes. Well, the thing about German is once it's with you, it stays with you. It's like riding ein bike.
jokes.setup: Deputy PM explaining what he said
jokes.punchline: Ben doubling down with mixed-language cliche
jokes.explanation: Ben's desperate attempt to save face with mangled German

jokes.text: I'll tell you how I know, because I remember when I first saw it, thinking to myself, 'What a hideous hat.' It is like she stuck her head in a swan and it's exploded.
jokes.setup: Identifying Selina's hat to prove she's in chapel
jokes.punchline: Brutal description of hat as exploded swan
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual metaphor, memorably vicious and quotable

jokes.text: Well, du lugst. Hilarious. I know.
jokes.setup: Deputy PM calling Ben a liar in German
jokes.punchline: Ben's fake confident response to being insulted
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of language deception, Ben still clueless

jokes.text: Okay, so unelected loony has the ear and minge of the vice president.
jokes.setup: Ray's influence over Selina
jokes.punchline: Crude British slang reducing relationship to anatomy
jokes.explanation: Crude British humor, functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: All of America is fat people. That's all we have. All of our children weigh 300 fucking pounds.
jokes.setup: Discussing how Ray's fat comments will upset Americans
jokes.punchline: Jonah's hyperbolic self-roast of American obesity
jokes.explanation: Decent dark observation about American obesity crisis

jokes.text: We'll get photos of her being surrounded by fat kids dressed as devils.
jokes.setup: Planning photo op to embarrass Selina
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific visual of costumed children protest
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific absurd image for political ambush

jokes.text: Way to go, Dan. Way to go, Ray. Yeah, just throw the blame around. Not at me, though. It's not my fault.
jokes.setup: Crisis emerging at press conference
jokes.punchline: Kent immediately deflecting blame while complaining about blame
jokes.explanation: Good Kent hypocrisy moment, in character self-protection

jokes.text: Yeah, well, Buddha's got a big fat ass, Ray.
jokes.setup: Ray comparing his beliefs to Buddha
jokes.punchline: Pointing out Buddha's physical appearance ironically
jokes.explanation: Perfect Amy comeback that destroys Ray's philosophical pretensions

jokes.text: I write a lot of 'treatii.' 'Treatises,' Ray. The normal plural.
jokes.setup: Ray trying to sound intellectual about his writings
jokes.punchline: Making up fake Latin plural, immediately corrected
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ray pseudo-intellectualism with Kent's sharp correction

jokes.text: Text the details to my cell... mobile.
jokes.setup: Jonah making dinner plans with British source
jokes.punchline: Awkward self-correction to British terminology
jokes.explanation: Typical Jonah trying too hard to fit in culturally

jokes.text: I have always been a friend of the fa... full-figured folks.
jokes.setup: Selina trying to distance herself from Ray's fat comments
jokes.punchline: Nearly saying 'fat' before awkward euphemism
jokes.explanation: Good Selina damage control moment, almost saying offensive thing

jokes.text: Get your hands off me, all right? I don't want to catch your fucking idiocy.
jokes.setup: Dan having breakdown, people trying to help
jokes.punchline: Treating stupidity like contagious disease
jokes.explanation: Good Dan superiority complex even during breakdown

jokes.text: Is that that egg with the sausage wrapped around it? Never mind. Not important.
jokes.setup: Dan mentioning Scotch egg causing his illness
jokes.punchline: Amy getting distracted by food description during crisis
jokes.explanation: Good Amy moment getting sidetracked by random detail

jokes.text: Yeah, they think folks who are physical can't be cerebral. They think those two things are separate, that they're not symbiotic.
jokes.setup: Kent explaining Ray's problem to him
jokes.punchline: Ray completely misunderstanding, thinking it's about stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ray missing the point entirely, great character blindness

jokes.text: You know, the brain is a muscle. No, it isn't.
jokes.setup: Ray trying to sound knowledgeable about anatomy
jokes.punchline: Kent's flat contradiction of obvious nonsense
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kent deadpan correction, economical and devastating

jokes.text: I don't panic. I'm the fucking iceman. That's why I'm the campaign manager.
jokes.setup: Dan clearly having panic attack
jokes.punchline: Claiming to be cool under pressure while panicking
jokes.explanation: Great Dan delusion during obvious meltdown, perfect irony

jokes.text: This is like the Queen's china. They're never gonna let us back into England again.
jokes.setup: Gary breaking expensive teacup during crisis
jokes.punchline: Amy treating diplomatic relations like dinner party etiquette
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of minor accident to international incident

jokes.text: You really don't get it, so I'm going to keep this very simple. You're fired. I get you. Very simple.
jokes.setup: Kent trying to fire Ray in clear terms
jokes.punchline: Ray still completely missing the point
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ray obliviousness even to direct termination

jokes.text: Fat people don't even vote. They can't even be bothered to get out of the house, you know? There's no food in the voting booth.
jokes.setup: Gary saying people love weight loss stories
jokes.punchline: Selina's brutal political calculation about overweight voters
jokes.explanation: Darkly perfect Selina political cynicism, memorable and quotable

jokes.text: And there's five fat kids outside dressed as Satan.
jokes.setup: Kent delivering more bad news
jokes.punchline: The reporter's absurd photo op threat has materialized
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to absurd threat becoming reality

jokes.text: You ask what temperature it is, you get some tiny little number. I don't know what... What? What is that supposed to mean? Even the plugs here are bigger than the things that they power.
jokes.setup: Selina ranting about hating Britain
jokes.punchline: Confusion about Celsius and absurd electrical outlet complaint
jokes.explanation: Good Selina cultural complaints, rambling but characteristic

jokes.text: I just got 'Brit-fucked' by that balloon animal.
jokes.setup: Selina realizing Ray caused her problems
jokes.punchline: Combining nationality with crude verb, calling Ray balloon animal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Selina coinage, creative and quotable insult

jokes.text: I mean, my God, Dan only hired him as a sex slave. What? Dan, he hired Ray as a... as a sex slave, did you not know?
jokes.setup: Gary explaining Ray's hiring
jokes.punchline: Casually revealing Dan's sexual motivation
jokes.explanation: Great revelation escalating the scandal, perfect Gary timing

jokes.text: He... he pimped me out?
jokes.setup: Learning Dan hired Ray as her sex partner
jokes.punchline: Selina processing being treated like prostitute
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina reaction to sexual manipulation revelation

jokes.text: I gotta say, Amy. You're like an actual friend. I mean, throughout all this... you know, me being made campaign manager over you... I mean, you could have been a real bitch, and you've been great. Well, I guess I'm a team player.
jokes.setup: Dan being grateful to Amy while hospitalized
jokes.punchline: Amy's dry response after he just called her a potential bitch
jokes.explanation: Good Dan backhanded compliment with Amy's understated response

jokes.text: No, those are for my 'filthy Mary Poppins' and I'm gonna give her a 'spoonful of sugar.'
jokes.setup: Amy asking if flowers are for Dan
jokes.punchline: Sexual reference to earlier Mary Poppins comparison
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier crude joke, predictable sexual reference

jokes.text: Selina just fired you as campaign manager, so take as long as you need. Take longer.
jokes.setup: Jonah pretending to care about Dan's health
jokes.punchline: Revealing firing news and encouraging longer recovery
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah fake sympathy turning into cruelty

jokes.text: You have put me in a hideous situation. Remotely, I might add. So from now on, remotely is how we will interact.
jokes.setup: Sue forced to cancel Prince Charles meeting
jokes.punchline: Declaring permanent remote-only relationship
jokes.explanation: Perfect Sue setting boundaries, escalating to permanent punishment

jokes.text: Running machines don't go anywhere.
jokes.setup: Told to get on running machine and run away
jokes.punchline: Ray's literal interpretation of treadmill mechanics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ray literal-mindedness, economical and in character

jokes.text: I couldn't help but overhear, but I always thought that lady was a little damaged.
jokes.setup: Ray commenting on First Lady's suicide attempt
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of mental health crisis
jokes.explanation: Ray's inappropriate response to tragedy, in character but uncomfortable

jokes.text: Okay, let's get the merrie olde fuck out of merrie olde England. I need to be driven to the airport at 'Diana speed,' okay? Just more carefully, though, please.
jokes.setup: Selina wanting to leave Britain quickly
jokes.punchline: Princess Diana car crash reference with immediate clarification
jokes.explanation: Dark Selina reference with immediate backtrack, quotable and memorable

jokes.text: All right. 'Dan Egan, 67, female.' Unemployed campaign manager. '30 different types of semen pumped from stomach, inverted nipples, abnormally high douche readings... chronic cretinism, leprosy, anal bleeding... tiny child balls'?
jokes.setup: Doctor reading Dan's medical chart
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd and insulting fake medical conditions
jokes.explanation: Hilariously escalating fake medical conditions, memorable ending

tmdb_overview: In the U.K. for the 100th anniversary of WWI, Selina’s staff worries about Ray’s growing influence on the Veep. Meanwhile, Dan’s nerves begin to fray as Ben gets news that sends him back to D.C. Later, Jonah arrives in London, U.K. to spy for Team Maddox, and Amy leaks a secret.
insight: 102 jokes in 39 minutes — Veep's densest episode sustains relentless character comedy.
